findings.title: Insights 'Worth today' silently wrong for ~30 live-only currencies (SEK/NOK/DKK/THB/TRY/PLN…) because Model-A vs Model-B hinges on a non-reactive, fire-and-forget FX-overlay race
findings.rootCause: The Model-A (has-FX) vs Model-B (no-FX) branch is decided by hasRate(curUp) (presentValue.ts:240). hasRate() only returns true for EUR + CONVERSION_RATES (17 codes) + the live overlay _liveRates (currency.ts:88-93). The live overlay is populated by refreshFxRates() which is fired fire-and-forget at boot (main.ts:106, `void refreshFxRates()`) and, on success, calls setLiveFxRates() which mutates a MODULE-PRIVATE object and does NOT emit STATE_CHANGED (currency.ts:62-69). The Insights useMemo depends on rateCache/cpiCache/etc. but never on the live overlay, so (a) before the overlay lands hasRate('SEK')=false → Model B is used, and (b) even after it lands the memo does not recompute, and (c) if /api/fx-rates fails outright the wrong model persists for the whole session. The hero gate (heroCalculating, Insights.tsx:563) only waits on the CPI fetch, never on the live overlay, so the wrong figure is shown as final. The historical 'Spent' leg uses rateCache (Frankfurter, which DOES carry SEK), so only the Today leg is wrong → leg mismatch.

findings.title: 'Add currency' control in the rates editor is mislabeled: button reuses the 'Add year' i18n key
findings.rootCause: RatesEditor.tsx:786 uses {t('settings.ratesAddYear')} for the add-currency button inside the add-currency group; the dedicated label key is ratesAddCurrencyLabel and there is no shared neutral 'Add' action key intended for both. The add-year button at line 815 correctly uses ratesAddYear.

findings.title: Auto-fill FX suggestion for a PAST year on a non-EUR home mixes at-the-time EUR cost with today's EUR→home rate
findings.rootCause: computeAutoRate() mode 'fx' computes homeValue = convertFn(euro,'EUR',homeCur) where euro is the at-the-time euroValue but convertFn only knows today's rate (manualRates.ts:186-188). implied = homeValue/value then becomes a year-rate suggestion. There is no historical EUR→home factor applied, so for home≠EUR the implied rate blends two epochs.

findings.title: Top-spender amount renders bare '0' (no currency symbol) when total display spend is zero
findings.rootCause: Insights.tsx:1245 ternary `{totalDisplay > 0 ? targetSym + formatNumberForCurrency(topSpenderAmount, targetCurr) : '0'}` emits the literal string '0' instead of the formatted/symboled value in the zero case.
